items.title: US says it struck Iranian radar sites as Iran targets American forces in Kuwait
items.summary: Iran and the US say they have carried out fresh strikes around the Strait of Hormuz, with Kuwait condemning "repeated" Iranian attacks.

items.title: Iran attacks damage 20 US military sites since start of war, satellite images show
items.summary: Analysts told BBC Verify that Tehran's strikes were more extensive than publicly acknowledged.

items.title: Israel PM orders strikes on Beirut suburbs as Hezbollah conflict escalates
items.summary: Benjamin Netanyahu says the Hezbollah stronghold of Dahieh will be targeted in response to attacks on Israeli civilians.

items.title: Huge blast kills dozens in rebel-held village in Myanmar
items.summary: Insurgents say it was caused by explosives being used for mining close to the Chinese border.

items.title: Five patients recover from Ebola in DR Congo and leave hospital
items.summary: Those infected can get better and officials stress that people should seek medical help if they have symptoms.

items.title: France seized sanctioned Russian oil tanker with UK help, Macron says
items.summary: A British helicopter provided support during the operation on Sunday, the UK ministry of defence says.

items.title: Poland's controversial 'Highway to Hel' 666 bus revived
items.summary: Poland's seaside resort called Hel is getting its 666 bus service back, despite earlier opposition from Christian groups.

items.title: Litterbugs now face on-the-spot fines in Tokyo's tourist hotspot
items.summary: Dozens of officials will patrol world-famous Shibuya as Japan responds to the impacts of overtourism.

items.title: Ethiopia election under way, but not everyone can vote
items.summary: Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed's party is expected to dominate the election as conflicts overshadow the vote.

items.title: Colombia presidential runoff pits leftist senator against pro-Trump rival
items.summary: Left-wing senator Iván Cepeda will face Trump admirer Abelardo de la Espriella at the final ballot on 21 June.

items.title: South Africa made to look like fools over World Cup visa issues, says minister
items.summary: After a day of uncertainty, the government confirms the team will depart for Mexico on Monday.

items.title: Hundreds arrested and dozens of police injured after Champions League riots in France
items.summary: Nearly 800 people were arrested after clashes with police which saw 219 people injured, including 57 police officers.

items.title: Dead whale towed ashore in Denmark ahead of autopsy
items.summary: "Timmy" was found dead off the coast of Anholt despite multiple attempts to rescue it.

items.title: Nicaraguan indigenous leader dies after three years in prison
items.summary: Rights groups condemn the death of Brooklyn Rivera, 73, following his long arbitrary detention by the governing regime.

items.title: Laos cave survivors help with plan to find last two missing men
items.summary: So far, five men have been freed from the narrow, flooded cave but efforts are ongoing to locate two more.

items.title: Trump seeking edits to US-Iran deal, US media report
items.summary: The requested changes are related to the Strait of Hormuz and the removal of highly enriched uranium, according to US media.

items.title: Japan defence minister denies militarism and criticises China's 'huge arsenal'
items.summary: Shinjiro Koizumi makes some of Tokyo's most pointed remarks yet in the dispute over Japan's military build-up.

items.title: Brazil monitors two patients for possible Ebola infection
items.summary: If confirmed, they would be the first infection cases outside Africa, since the outbreak began in DR Congo.

items.title: 'Cancel it', Trump says after artists drop out of US Freedom 250 festival
items.summary: Trump suggests holding a "Make America Great Again" rally instead after most headliners drop out of White-House-linked event.

items.title: A year of grief after Air India crash: What remains when a plane falls from the sky
items.summary: A mother still speaks about her son who died in the crash in the present tense and a brother waits for answers.

items.title: The drivers risking death on Ukraine's most dangerous bus routes
items.summary: Russian drones are targeting public buses in Kherson, killing three transport workers so far this year.

items.title: Grammy-winning director explores his Nigerian grandfather's role in the Biafran war
items.summary: Filmmaker Meji Alabi directs a landmark BBC Africa Eye documentary about Nigeria's civil war.

items.summary: China's worst coal mining disaster in 15 years comes amid an ambitious pivot towards green energy.

items.title: Caribbean hot sauce producers warn of shortages and higher prices
items.summary: Manufacturers in Jamaica say the key chilli peppers they need are in limited supply.

items.title: How Putin became master of the image
items.summary: From enigmatic KGB agent to wartime ruler, this is how Putin has repeatedly reinvented his image, and himself.

items.title: Cuba’s blackouts leave high-rise residents with constant uncertainty
items.summary: Will Grant spoke to a 70-year-old widow who says the inability to use her building's elevator during a power outage trapped her and her husband when he needed medical care.

items.title: Ferrari wanted to take on Chinese EVs with the Luce - then the backlash started
items.summary: The brand's first EV has been heavily criticised, with some saying it has abandoned Ferrari's roots.

items.title: Trump's doctor says he's in excellent health - but are US presidents' health checks a PR exercise?
items.summary: Every president in modern history - including Donald Trump this week - has taken an annual physical exam that is as much about messaging as it is about health.

items.title: Marilyn Monroe 'lookalikes' gather to celebrate her 100th birthday

items.title: 'I will sleep with fear': Romanians shaken after block of flats hit by drone
